notes: Primary AI vendor in the arc — Deep Blue (1997), MedSpeak (1997), Intelligent Miner (1997), Watson (2011), watsonx (2023); most diverse pre-2000 AI portfolio in the archive
Oracle InterOffice document summarization (1995) is the archive's highest-specificity contemporary AI precursor claim; Oracle 23ai (2024) closes the arc for the same company
AI hardware arc: Gaudi AI accelerator (2019) and Falcon Shores (2025); zero pre-2010 AI product claims in archive; enables AI rather than building it
XCON expert system (1980) — the archive's deepest AI anchor; DEC deployed XCON in production for VAX configuration; also referenced in MYCIN clinical AI context
MYCIN (1972–1980) developed at Stanford; rule-based medical diagnosis expert system; cited in Kastner Long View memoir as commercially significant expert system of the 1980s
Tandem ORDM (1997) — Object Relational Data Mining Architecture; embedded ML algorithms in SQL database; direct architectural precursor to BigQuery ML / Oracle 23ai in-database ML

Microsoft Word AutoSummarize (1997–2010) — mass-market extractive document summarization; removed in Word 2010; resurrected as Copilot (2023); not directly in Kastner archive but most important companion observation
ChatGPT (2022) and GPT-4 (2023) — the LLM-era convergence endpoint for all threads in the arc; provides the 2022 baseline year for predictive distance calculations
AlphaGo (2016) and AlphaFold (2020) as LLM-era endpoint references for the Deep Blue / chess AI thread; Transformer architecture (2017, Google Brain) as the LLM convergence enabler
Primary archive author; MIT PhD (AI-adjacent environment 1966–1972); observed Oracle InterOffice (1995) and IBM AI portfolio (1997) directly; noted InterOffice as 'first hint of AI routine to come'
Publishing vehicle for Kastner's Oracle and IBM AI product assessments 1993–2006; Aberdeen Data Mining Buying Guide (1997) is the archive's densest pre-2000 ML cluster
Lotus Notes agents (1989) — scripted background automation on document databases; earliest agent architecture in the archive; Aberdeen (1995) critiqued Notes as 'first-generation' groupware
EPM/planning analytics vendor; acquired by Oracle 2007; represents the BI-to-AI transition thread in financial analytics
Retail data mining specialist; Aberdeen 1997 study documented KD1 Retail Discovery Suite as democratizing data mining for retail professionals — removing specialist dependency
BusinessMiner (1997) — $995 data mining add-on to BusinessObjects analytics; Aberdeen documented as precursor to democratized ML; acquired by SAP 2008
MYCIN (1972) medical diagnosis expert system; alias for stanford-heuristic-lab used in Thread 1 observations
XCON expert system (1980-1993); alias for digital-equipment-corporation used in Thread 1 observations
GPT-3 (2020), ChatGPT (2022), GPT-4 (2023); LLM convergence endpoint for all arc threads
